meta.description: Canonical Phenome Registry — shared functional outcome domains above mechanisms, below condition profiles. Therapeutic areas tag where each phenome is clinically relevant; ADHD (TA001) is the primary Version 1 validation use case. Assignment pipeline: system/phenome-relationship-schema.md#phenome-assignment-pipeline.
meta.phenomeDevelopment: Candidate phenomes were developed within the BRAIN Framework as functional outcome domains linking biological regulatory systems to clinically relevant cognitive, behavioural, emotional, social, and physiological capacities. During development, the registry was compared with established research frameworks, including the NIH Research Domain Criteria (RDoC), to identify omissions, reduce overlap between phenomes, and improve construct separation. These external frameworks were used to validate and refine the taxonomy rather than define its structure. The BRAIN Phenome Registry therefore represents an independent translational classification informed by, but not derived from, existing neuropsychiatric research frameworks.

benchmarkedFrameworks.name: NIH Research Domain Criteria
benchmarkedFrameworks.note: Used to identify omissions, reduce phenome overlap, and improve construct separation — not the defining taxonomy.

benchmarkedFrameworks.name: WHO International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health
benchmarkedFrameworks.note: Optional cross-reference for activity and participation domains where clinically relevant.

therapeuticAreas.name: ADHD
therapeuticAreas.description: Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder and related executive-function, impulsivity, and hyperactivity phenotypes.

therapeuticAreas.name: Anxiety Disorders
therapeuticAreas.description: Generalised anxiety, social anxiety, panic, and related hyperarousal and worry phenotypes.

therapeuticAreas.name: Depressive Disorders
therapeuticAreas.description: Major depression, dysthymia, and related motivational, affective, and cognitive slowing phenotypes.

therapeuticAreas.name: Autism Spectrum
therapeuticAreas.description: Autism spectrum conditions and related sensory, social-cognitive, and regulatory phenotypes.

therapeuticAreas.name: Cognitive Ageing
therapeuticAreas.description: Age-related cognitive decline, mild cognitive impairment, and dementia-risk cognitive phenotypes.

therapeuticAreas.name: Long COVID
therapeuticAreas.description: Post-acute infection syndromes with cognitive, fatigue, and autonomic phenotypes.

therapeuticAreas.name: Healthy Cognitive Optimisation
therapeuticAreas.description: Non-clinical cognitive performance, resilience, and brain-health optimisation contexts.

reviewFlags.note: Stress Resilience (adaptive capacity) vs Stress Reactivity (response intensity) — related but not identical; do not merge automatically.
Motivation/Drive vs Behavioural Activation — related; remain separate unless manually merged.
Emotional Regulation (affective modulation) vs Apprehensive Worry (sustained apprehensive/ruminative cognition) — RDoC Negative Valence / cognitive control overlap; remain separate.

Reward Regulation (sensitivity, learning, seeking) vs Pleasure & Interest Capacity (experienced interest/pleasure and consummatory engagement) — RDoC Positive Valence / anhedonia domain; remain separate.
Emotional Regulation vs Social Engagement Capacity (affiliation, approach, participation) — RDoC Social Processes; remain separate.

phenomes.name: Focus / Attention Stability
phenomes.description: Capacity to sustain, shift, and regulate attention across changing cognitive demands.
phenomes.publicSummary: How steadily a person can hold, shift, and return attention when tasks or distractions change.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: ADHD-focused construct and precursor-transport biology are well represented; human dietary attention-outcome evidence on the registry stack is indirect (omega-3 cognition trials) — not dietary ADHD treatment efficacy.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 ADHD registry phenome (pre-v3). Benchmarked against RDoC Cognitive Systems (attention, cognitive control) and distinguished from Cognitive Clarity (PH011) as sustained/shifted attention versus processing clarity.

rdoc_domains: Cognitive Systems — attention
Cognitive Systems — cognitive control

construct_landmark_papers.label: Millichap & Yee (2012)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Reviews diet and nutritional factors in ADHD — foundational construct framing for attention phenotypes.

construct_landmark_papers.label: Aquili (2020)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Reviews catecholaminergic neurotransmission and attentional function relevant to ADHD biology.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Fernstrom (2013)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Large neutral amino acid transport and precursor availability intersect monoaminergic attention pathways.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Derbyshire & Maes (2023)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Links oxidative stress and neuroinflammation biology to ADHD symptom domains including attention.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Wang et al. (2019)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Dietary patterns and amino-acid context modulate neurotransmitter precursor biology upstream of attention.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Oulhaj et al. (2016)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Omega-3 supplementation associated with cognitive performance improvements in ageing — adjacent human nutrition→cognition support.

phenomes.name: Motivation / Drive
phenomes.description: Capacity to initiate effort, pursue goals, and maintain goal-directed behaviour.
phenomes.publicSummary: How readily a person starts effort, pursues goals, and stays engaged with what matters to them.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Dopaminergic and endocannabinoid motivation biology are review-supported; open-label tyrosine and dietary precursor evidence is mechanistic or adjunct — not definitive motivation-intervention efficacy.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 ADHD registry phenome. Benchmarked against RDoC Positive Valence Systems (motivation, reward learning) and kept distinct from Behavioural Activation (PH010) and Reward Regulation (PH009).

rdoc_domains: Positive Valence Systems — reward learning
Positive Valence Systems — motivation / effort

construct_landmark_papers.label: MacDonald et al. (2024)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Reviews dopamine signalling in motivation and goal-directed behaviour.

construct_landmark_papers.label: Aquili (2020)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Catecholaminergic neurotransmission framing for effort and drive biology.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Fernstrom (2013)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Precursor transport and tyrosine availability upstream of catecholaminergic drive circuits.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Garani et al. (2021)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Endocannabinoid system modulation of motivation and stress-related behavioural states.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Wurtman et al. (2003)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Dietary tyrosine and precursor manipulation affects catecholamine synthesis biology.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Reimherr et al. (1987)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Open-label tyrosine supplementation in ADHD — early human adjunct evidence for drive/activation biology.

phenomes.name: Emotional Regulation
phenomes.description: Capacity to modulate emotional responses and maintain affective stability.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well emotional responses can be modulated so mood and reactivity stay within a workable range.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Construct and inhibitory-balance biology are strong; registry nutrition layer draws on mood/anxiety intervention reviews — not direct emotional-regulation phenome trials.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 ADHD registry phenome. Benchmarked against RDoC Negative Valence and Arousal/Regulatory Systems; distinct from Apprehensive Worry (PH016), Stress Reactivity (PH015), and Social Engagement (PH018).

rdoc_domains: Negative Valence Systems — sustained affect / affective regulation
Arousal and Regulatory Systems — affective regulation

construct_landmark_papers.label: Oades et al. (2010)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Reviews dopamine and serotonin roles in motivation, affect, and ADHD-related emotional function.

construct_landmark_papers.label: Edden et al. (2012)
construct_landmark_papers.note: GABA deficits in ADHD cohorts — construct validation for excitation–inhibition and affective control.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Fernstrom (2013)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Serotonergic precursor biology intersects affective modulation pathways.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Briguglio et al. (2018)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Dietary neurotransmitter biology review spanning mood and anxiety-relevant serotonergic context.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Kiecolt-Glaser et al. (2011)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Omega-3 lowered anxiety and inflammatory markers in stressed adults — upstream affective biology.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Jackson et al. (2021)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Saffron RCT reported mood improvements — adjacent human nutrition→affective biology support.

phenomes.name: Cognitive Energy Stability
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain mental energy and avoid cognitive energy crashes across the day.
phenomes.publicSummary: How evenly mental energy holds up through the day without sharp crashes or fog.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Mitochondrial and metabolic-flexibility reviews anchor the construct; micronutrient and sex-hormone links are mechanistic — limited direct cognitive-fatigue outcome demonstration on the registry stack.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 registry phenome for mental energy stability across the day. Distinguished from Energy Stability Under Variable Conditions (PH012) and fuel-substrate phenome (PH013) as cognitive energy tone rather than metabolic supply mechanics.

rdoc_domains: Arousal and Regulatory Systems — fatigue / energy regulation
Cognitive Systems — cognitive effort

phenomes.name: Recovery Capacity
phenomes.description: Capacity to restore function after cognitive, emotional, metabolic, or physical load.
phenomes.publicSummary: How effectively function returns after sustained cognitive, emotional, metabolic, or physical demand.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Recovery biology spans redox, NAD⁺, and metabolic-flexibility reviews; human nutrition evidence is supportive but not recovery-phenome-specific RCTs.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 registry phenome for post-load functional restoration. Related to Stress Resilience (PH006) and Metabolic Resilience (PH007) but targets recovery trajectory rather than buffering or substrate flexibility alone.

phenomes.name: Stress Resilience
phenomes.description: Capacity to buffer, adapt to, and recover from stress exposure.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well the system buffers stress, adapts under pressure, and returns toward baseline afterward.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Endocannabinoid and stress-adaptation reviews are strong; human dietary stress-resilience outcomes are indirect (omega-3 anxiety, oatmeal preclinical adjunct).

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 registry phenome for adaptive stress buffering. Benchmarked against RDoC Negative Valence and Arousal systems; distinct from Stress Reactivity (PH015) per RF002.

rdoc_domains: Negative Valence Systems — sustained threat / stress adaptation
Arousal and Regulatory Systems — stress regulation

phenomes.name: Metabolic Resilience
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain function under changing metabolic, energetic, or physiological demand.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well the body and brain maintain stable function when energy demand, fuel availability, or metabolic conditions change.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Metabolic-flexibility and ketone-body reviews anchor the construct; ketone intervention human evidence supports substrate biology more than broad metabolic-resilience phenome endpoints.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 registry phenome for maintaining function under changing metabolic demand. Overlaps conceptually with PH012/PH013 but emphasises systemic adaptive capacity rather than condition-specific energy stability labels.

rdoc_domains: Arousal and Regulatory Systems — metabolic / physiological regulation
icf_domains: Body functions — metabolic and energy production functions

phenomes.name: Sleep / Calming Tone
phenomes.description: Capacity to downshift arousal and support sleep-compatible physiological regulation.
phenomes.publicSummary: How readily arousal can downshift toward calmer, sleep-compatible physiological states.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: GABAergic sleep biochemistry is mechanistically strong (Cataldo); human dietary sleep-outcome evidence on the registry stack is limited — not sleep-disorder treatment efficacy.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 registry phenome for arousal downshift and sleep-compatible regulation. Benchmarked against RDoC Arousal/Regulatory Systems (sleep–wake, circadian).

phenomes.publicSummary: How reward sensitivity, reinforcement learning, and reward-seeking behaviour are regulated over time.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Endocannabinoid reward biology is review-supported but sparse on mechanism pages; registry stack lacks direct human reward-regulation outcome trials.

phenomes.publicSummary: How effectively motivation converts into starting tasks and sustaining behavioural engagement.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Mechanism coverage is thin at registry level; endocannabinoid and dopaminergic reviews provide construct context only — not behavioural-activation intervention evidence.

phenomes.name: Cognitive Clarity
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain clear, organised mental processing without excessive fog or confusion.
phenomes.publicSummary: How clear and organised thinking feels — distinct from attention stability or raw mental energy.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Oxidative-stress and omega-3 cognition reviews support biology→phenome; homocysteine and gut–estrogen links are mechanistic — clarity outcomes not isolated as primary endpoints on the stack.

provenance.developmentNote: Core Version 1 registry phenome for mental processing clarity (distinct from Focus/Attention Stability PH001 and Cognitive Energy PH004). Enriched with cross-system oxidative and micronutrient biology.

phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Metabolic-flexibility reviews strongly anchor the construct; single-FM mechanism linkage in BRAIN v1 — nutrition layer is micronutrient/bioenergetic context rather than condition-variable energy trials.

phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Ketone and glucose-efficiency human studies support fuel-switching biology; registry stack does not demonstrate dietary fuel-manipulation trials as energy-phenome primary endpoints.

phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Estrobolome and sex-hormone signalling reviews are mechanistically strong; human dietary hormone-stability trials are not represented on the foundational stack.

phenomes.name: Apprehensive Worry / Perseverative Thought
phenomes.description: Capacity to limit sustained apprehensive worry, rumination, and difficulty disengaging from threat-anticipatory or negative perseverative thought.
phenomes.publicSummary: How much worry, rumination, or hard-to-stop apprehensive thinking intrudes on everyday function — distinct from general emotional reactivity or stress-response intensity.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Foundational stack combines mechanistic construct validation (Briguglio, Marsland) with translational biology→phenome and human intervention support (Kiecolt-Glaser) — not direct dietary treatment efficacy for worry disorders.

provenance.developmentNote: Introduced in registry v3 (2026) following cross-TA anxiety/depression gap analysis. Benchmarked against RDoC Negative Valence Systems (potential threat) and Cognitive Systems (perseverative thought / cognitive control) to remain distinct from Emotional Regulation (PH003) and Stress Reactivity (PH015).

rdoc_domains: Negative Valence Systems — potential threat (anxiety)
Cognitive Systems — cognitive control / perseverative thought
dsm_icd_context: Major depressive disorder — rumination / worry features

construct_landmark_papers.label: Briguglio et al. (2018)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Reviews dietary neurotransmitter biology (including serotonin) relevant to neuropsychiatric anxiety and mood contexts.

construct_landmark_papers.label: Marsland et al. (2017)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Links systemic inflammation to default-mode network connectivity patterns associated with perseverative thought.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Marsland et al. (2017)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Neuroimmune signalling intersects default-mode hyperconnectivity relevant to worry/rumination biology.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Batey et al. (2024)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: LPS-linked neurotransmission disruption in depression — adjacent biology for inflammatory–affective perseveration context.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Kiecolt-Glaser et al. (2011)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Omega-3 supplementation lowered anxiety and inflammatory markers in stressed adults.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Zelicha et al. (2022)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Polyphenol-rich dietary pattern reduced inflammatory tone — upstream context for neuroimmune worry biology.

phenomes.name: Pleasure & Interest Capacity
phenomes.description: Capacity to experience interest, anticipatory pleasure, and consummatory engagement with rewarding or meaningful activities — the functional domain overlapping anhedonia in depressive and related conditions.
phenomes.publicSummary: How readily interest and pleasure in activities can be felt and sustained — distinct from raw motivation to start tasks or reward-seeking behaviour.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Construct and biology→phenome layers are review- and mechanistic-heavy; Jackson (2021) adds human intervention support for mood/pleasure domains without isolating anhedonia as a primary endpoint.

provenance.developmentNote: Introduced in registry v3 (2026) following cross-TA depression gap analysis. Benchmarked against RDoC Positive Valence Systems (reward valuation, consummatory pleasure / anhedonia) to remain distinct from Reward Regulation (PH009), Motivation/Drive (PH002), and Behavioural Activation (PH010).

rdoc_domains: Positive Valence Systems — reward valuation
Positive Valence Systems — initial response to reward / consummatory pleasure

construct_landmark_papers.label: Gruber et al. (2023)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Reviews insulin–dopamine reward-processing disruption in depression pathophysiology.

construct_landmark_papers.label: Lopresti & Drummond (2014)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Systematic review of saffron clinical studies and antidepressant mechanisms — pleasure/mood construct validation.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Gruber et al. (2023)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Brain dopamine signalling and reward processing as underexplored depression mechanisms.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Song et al. (2023)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Mitochondrial dysfunction in depression — adjacent biology for reward-energy integration.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Jackson et al. (2021)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Saffron RCT reported reduced depression scores and improved social well-being in subclinical mood contexts.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Ferguson et al. (2014)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Omega-3 PUFA modulates inflammatory and resolution-phase biology intersecting mood pathways.

phenomes.name: Social Engagement Capacity
phenomes.description: Capacity for affiliative approach, social participation, and maintained engagement in interpersonal contexts without excessive withdrawal or avoidance.
phenomes.publicSummary: How readily a person can approach, participate in, and stay engaged with social connection — distinct from general emotional regulation alone.
phenomes.evidence_confidence_note: Gut–brain and social-rank biology are strongly preclinical; Jackson (2021) provides adjacent human social-wellbeing outcome support — not a direct social-engagement phenome trial.

provenance.developmentNote: Introduced in registry v3 (2026) following cross-TA anxiety/depression and autism-spectrum gap analysis. Benchmarked against RDoC Social Processes (affiliation, attachment, social communication) to remain distinct from Emotional Regulation (PH003).

rdoc_domains: Social Processes — affiliation and attachment
Social Processes — social communication
dsm_icd_context: Autism spectrum disorder — social communication differences

construct_landmark_papers.label: Hollis et al. (2015)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Trait anxiety predisposes to social subordination; nucleus accumbens mitochondrial function mediates social rank biology.

construct_landmark_papers.label: Bravo et al. (2011)
construct_landmark_papers.note: Gut microbiota modulates central GABA receptors and anxiety/depression-related behaviour via gut–brain signalling.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Bravo et al. (2011)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Microbiota–GABAergic gut–brain pathway linked to anxiety-related social behaviour in preclinical models.

biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.label: Briguglio et al. (2018)
biology_to_phenome_landmark_papers.note: Dietary neurotransmitter review spanning serotonergic and GABAergic biology relevant to social-affective function.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Bravo et al. (2011)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Probiotic L. rhamnosus modulates gut–brain GABAergic signalling — dietary microbiome intervention context.

nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.label: Jackson et al. (2021)
nutrition_to_biology_landmark_papers.note: Saffron supplementation improved social relationship quality alongside mood outcomes in RCT.
